# Research Brief — Method-Gap Audit of Continuity Loom Author Playtest

Brief ID: CMGR-PLAYTEST-2026-07-18-01

## 0. Commission

- Audited skill: playtest; requested path .claude/skills/playtest; resolved target
  /home/joeloverbeck/projects/continuity-loom/.claude/skills/playtest/SKILL.md; real path is
  identical; declared version: unversioned.
- Repository scope: /home/joeloverbeck/projects/continuity-loom, selected because it is the
  nearest Git root to the target.
- Source identity: current SKILL.md SHA-256
  e37314da2f286c884e1888d9e984bddc623d5a375f3537301feee7b5102ac4ac; tracked blob
  1c462c3cd7b33a5df5694ee7481355b2f7454f31; repository baseline
  a3d6b4371f6f583f1964f414a4b34f76a12c22d1; last committed target change
  a1508fe10c86751fb6e77d662ee2c9f519bda921. The target tree has two live modified references,
  identified and inlined in Appendices B and C. Every other target file matches the baseline
  commit.
- Authority posture: .claude/skills/playtest is canonical.
  .agents/skills/playtest is a tracked symlink with blob
  6a4cb46a0319e9842d21deb0c8a6596f9a947480 and target
  ../../.claude/skills/playtest. It resolves to the canonical directory; it is not a second copy.
- Evidence-packet identity:
  CL-PLAYTEST-MG-20260718-a3d6b43-78223c30-ce4478df. Clean sources are verified at the public
  immutable commit a3d6b4371f6f583f1964f414a4b34f76a12c22d1. The two dirty target files are
  fully inlined as base64 with current SHA-256 hashes.
- Decision owner: repository owner and commissioning user.
- Commissioner: Codex applying commission-method-gap-research.
- Research executor: one ChatGPT-Pro deep-research session, or an equivalent external researcher
  with web and scholarly-source access. No external or paid research mechanism has been invoked.
- Audit date: 2026-07-18.
- Prior audit: never. No playtest-specific external method-gap brief, returned report,
  disposition, or adopted landing was found locally, in Git history, or in the GitHub issue
  tracker.
- In-flight or unconsumed work: none for playtest methodology. GitHub issues 97 and 98 concern the
  product's Author-Focused Ideation behavior and implementation spec, not this skill's research
  method. The untracked generic commissioning framework and commissioning skill do not identify
  playtest and are not target lineage.
- Internal-source access: verified direct public repository references plus fully inlined dirty
  supplements.
- External-source access: current public web, scholarly literature, standards, and primary
  sources available to the executor as of the research date. Decision-critical claims require
  source verification; inaccessible material must be marked relayed—unverified.
- Privacy and sanitization: do not reproduce or request API keys, full compiled prompts, full
  record payloads, project databases, candidate prose, accepted prose, or raw assistance
  responses. Use the public reports' fingerprints, counts, short excerpts, and privacy-safe
  evidence only.

This is a bounded, evidence-backed audit of missing method capabilities. It does not authorize
edits to the audited skill or adoption of recommendations.

### Census and cadence determination

Recommendation: **commission now**.

This is the first external method-gap audit. The ten-version cadence heuristic has not been met:
the package was created at 6645191ee73f41787757953232831cf692d0ac80, received one organic
method repair at a1508fe10c86751fb6e77d662ee2c9f519bda921, and now has two narrow live reference
edits. That heuristic is not controlling here. The baseline is useful to freeze because the
skill's intended outcomes and main instruments remained stable across one new-story run and one
continuation, while its empirical record now contains repeated stochastic-assistance divergence,
one report-schema repair, and enough evidence to distinguish established machinery from absent
controls. No prior or overlapping method research is in flight.

The explicit request to commission this audit authorizes this brief because the census recommends
commission now. It does not authorize running the research.

## 1. Objective

Identify materially consequential instrument classes, evidence bridges, controls, or decision
machinery that Continuity Loom Author Playtest needs but does not adequately possess. Compare
capabilities rather than terminology. Complement the skill's internal improvement loop; do not
replace it.

The audit target is the playtest method, not Continuity Loom product behavior. Product defects,
feature proposals, and open product issues may be interval evidence, but they are not
recommendations for this report to re-adjudicate.

This audit cannot establish that no further gaps exist.

## 2. Intended outcomes and internal improvement loop

### Intended outcomes

- Execute one sincere, blind-first or report-driven returning-author journey in the real visible
  app through exactly one accepted local prose segment.
- Observe strengths, friction, confusion, defects, blockers, and prompt behavior without using
  hidden state or changing the product during the run.
- Evaluate exact visibly inspected prose and assistance prompts with isolated cold-context model
  responses while making no OpenRouter request.
- Preserve the human-authority boundary by requiring visible candidate review, manual re-authoring,
  explicit acceptance, and deliberate post-acceptance continuity work.
- Produce a cumulative, privacy-safe, evidence-backed report that can support continuation and
  downstream product or skill decisions.
- Warrant strong safety and custody claims: localhost-only app, blank credentials, guarded
  provider routes, transient sensitive artifacts, and no unrelated repository mutation.

### Claim-instrument-evidence map

| Intended outcome and material claim | Current instrument and evidence artifact | Threshold or rule | Authority, failure route, and feedback route | Weak or blank cell to investigate |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First-use and returning-author experience is observed rather than reconstructed | Pre-use story intent and mental model; visible-only browser journey; contemporaneous observation scratchpad; correction entries; report findings and screenshots | Record meaningful first occurrences and expectation mismatches; classify severity by actual journey impact | The playtest agent observes and judges; bounded recovery or blocked report on terminal failure; findings flow to the report | The agent reads a detailed product-specific method before first navigation. There is no explicit staged-blindness or contamination-control instrument |
| Coverage remains sincere rather than box-ticking | Naturalistic-use rule; one exact story segment; explicit reasons for skipped surfaces; coverage-limitations section | Use a surface only when an author need arises; exactly one segment per invocation | The playtest agent selects the journey; continuation reads only the supplied report | No representative-task portfolio, cross-story coverage model, or saturation rule exists inside the skill |
| Visible findings distinguish fact, interpretation, and correction | Observation schema separates observed fact, author interpretation, expected versus actual, and later correction; diagnostics are allowed only after a visible concern | Use one primary classification, category, severity, confidence, and evidence route | The same agent records, interprets, and writes the final claim; downstream prep may refine a remedy | No independent claim-evidence check, rival-explanation review, or semantic report validator exists |
| Prompt source reaches the model-facing artifact as intended | Exact visible prompt extraction; prompt fingerprint; visible metadata; Generation Brief field-influence ledger; prompt search and section placement notes | Each populated prompt-facing field is present, absent, or unclear, then used, partly used, not compiled, not observably used, contradicted, or not assessable | The playtest agent adjudicates; one targeted counterfactual is allowed when useful | Attribution from prompt presence to response influence remains a single-observer judgment with no explicit oracle or inter-rater control |
| A response is useful and failures are attributed to prompt contract, model execution, source data, UI workflow, or uncertainty | Fresh cold-context subagent; structured usefulness verdict; one unchanged-prompt retry when materially poor; at most one one-variable counterfactual | Prose gets one or two attempts; counterfactuals are diagnostic only; verdicts include confidence and acknowledge stochastic limits | The playtest agent chooses retry, selected output, adoption, and likely layer; cold-context unavailability blocks evaluation | No predeclared stochastic sampling, sequential stopping, comparator, or uncertainty instrument supports reliability claims |
| Accepted prose remains a deliberate author choice | Visible candidate entry and edit; intervention-burden category; explicit Accept action; accepted-sequence readback | Neither of two prose attempts may require replacement-level rewriting; exactly one segment is accepted | The playtest agent acts as author and gatekeeper; failure becomes a prose-quality blocker | The burden and acceptance judgments have no independent or calibrated witness; actual human transfer is untested |
| Assistance output is useful without becoming authority | Surface-specific adoption/rejection ledgers for Ideate, Record Hygiene, and Segment Reconciliation; manual re-authoring in canonical editors | Natural invocation only; output remains outside provider result cards; adoption counts and false positives are recorded | The playtest agent adjudicates every suggestion; malformed or unavailable paths block or remain a coverage limitation | No common reliability design compares empty, partial, contradictory, and useful stochastic outputs across runs |
| Continuation preserves state and cumulative learning | Supplied prior report; project-path check; Accepted Segments readback; stable finding IDs; cumulative ledger; continuation handoff | Resolve only after explicit retest; preserve every inherited finding; read no unrelated report | Report chain is the authority for that story; missing temporary project blocks continuation | Learning is story-local. There is no privacy-safe cross-run evidence register, cadence trigger, or method-revision threshold |
| Provider, local-first, and repository custody claims are warranted | Blank OPENROUTER_API_KEY; isolated config; loopback-only app; guarded provider endpoints; exact-origin browser guard; worktree baseline/final comparison; evidence cleanup | Any provider attempt blocks; no hidden/API/project-file state; only report/evidence may change | Executable guards and validator enforce many rules; blocker report on failure | Safety machinery is strong. Research should test for equivalent coverage before proposing additions, not rename it |
| The final report is durable, inspectable, and privacy-safe | Fixed frontmatter, ordered sections, required tables, evidence index, secret/payload scans, evidence-directory rules, validator and tests | Validator must pass after sensitive scratch cleanup; current target tests must pass | Validator blocks publication; downstream prep and skill-audit are the observed repair path | Validator checks structure and selected privacy invariants, not whether every claim is semantically supported or whether confidence is warranted |

### Normal use to revision loop

The observed loop is:

1. prepare and run one browser journey;
2. append contemporaneous evidence and judgments;
3. validate and publish one cumulative report;
4. consume the report through PRD prep, product triage, or skill-audit;
5. land a product or method change through the normal repository process;
6. explicitly retest in a later playtest and update the cumulative ledger.

The first run demonstrates this loop for method conformance: its later PRD prep identified a
counterfactual-disclosure mismatch, and commit a1508fe added report-format instructions,
validator enforcement, and tests. The loop is observable but not yet explicitly governed by a
cross-run method ledger or trigger.

## 3. Operating envelope

- Maintainers and roles: one repository owner/decision maker is evidenced by the repository and
  tracker. Agent assistance is available. Do not assume a research operations team, standing
  study staff, or multiple routine adjudicators.
- One-time and recurring time: a normal run already occupies one bounded author journey of about
  eighty to ninety-six minutes in the two available reports. Recommendations must offer a
  trial-sized first witness. Any method that routinely requires a second full journey, a panel, or
  a separate long review must be deferred unless its marginal value is demonstrated.
- Compute, tools, and infrastructure: local Node 24 npm workspace; Git and GitHub; Playwright with
  Chromium/Chrome; localhost-only app helpers; temporary filesystem storage; fresh Claude or Codex
  cold-context subagents. No OpenRouter request is permitted during the playtest.
- Users, participants, and experts: no recruited author panel, accessibility specialist, domain
  expert, or external evaluator is assumed available. Recommendations may specify a participant
  witness, but must disposition it as deferred with a concrete recruitment trigger.
- Privacy and publication: the repository and immutable baseline are public. Story projects,
  exact prompts, raw responses, record payloads, candidate prose, accepted prose, API keys, and
  browser profiles remain local/transient and must not enter a research report.
- Maximum recurring ceremony: prefer at most one lightweight artifact, check, or bounded review
  added to a normal run. Do not import a full institutional usability, psychometric, assurance, or
  quality-management framework.
- Other non-negotiable constraints: preserve blind-first intent, visible-UI-only action, one
  accepted segment, no paid/provider calls, human gatekeeping, local-first custody, deterministic
  prompt inspection, report privacy, and the constitutional separation of records, prompts,
  accepted prose, and author-private notes.

Adapt every recommendation to this envelope. Do not import institutional frameworks whole.

## 4. Evidence packet

### Current skill and supporting machinery

Public immutable base:

https://github.com/joeloverbeck/continuity-loom/tree/a3d6b4371f6f583f1964f414a4b34f76a12c22d1

Read the following at that exact commit:

- AGENTS.md — repository safety, package, localhost, authority, and completion rules.
- CLAUDE.md — repository entrypoint and skill/tracker conventions.
- docs/ACTIVE-DOCS.md — active authority registry and archive boundary.
- docs/principles/FOUNDATIONS.md — constitutional boundaries relevant to prompt transparency, human
  authority, local-first custody, assistance quarantine, accepted prose, and secrets.
- .claude/skills/playtest/SKILL.md — target outcomes, non-negotiables, process, and completion.
- Every file under .claude/skills/playtest/references and
  .claude/skills/playtest/scripts listed in Appendix A — behavior, evidence, validators, and tests.
- .agents/skills/playtest — discovery symlink only; resolve it to the canonical package.

Then replace the two base-ref references with the exact current bytes in Appendices B and C:

- .claude/skills/playtest/references/browser-driver.md
- .claude/skills/playtest/references/prompt-evaluation.md

Those appendices are base64 because the source files contain nested Markdown fences. Decode each
block exactly and verify its SHA-256 before use.

### Empirical history

Read these at the immutable baseline:

- reports/playtest-the-unbidden-oath-2026-07-17T104952Z.md — first new-story execution; SHA-256
  b8bad94a76e92201e474b81c6a0780ba34a4e71dff2f9aa3563badb656f18694.
- reports/playtest-the-unbidden-oath-2026-07-17T104952Z-prd-prep.md — downstream adjudication and
  method-repair route; SHA-256
  4c1f1693c62998f14f4d5f8662fc61358c51616757914ab956aabf80c4563e51.
- reports/playtest-the-unbidden-oath-2026-07-18T145754Z.md — report-driven continuation and
  repeated stochastic evidence; SHA-256
  feec1508c1638abf978f302f942048f347bd9fce5ec7dcd7ab028288db76bd6d.
- Git commits 6645191ee73f41787757953232831cf692d0ac80 and
  a1508fe10c86751fb6e77d662ee2c9f519bda921 — creation baseline and first organic method landing.

Current executable evidence, observed on 2026-07-18:

- All 27 target-local Node tests passed.
- The second report passes the current validator.
- The first report now fails the current validator because it declares one counterfactual probe
  without the subsequently required Targeted Counterfactual disclosure. Treat this as honest
  lineage evidence, not a reason to rewrite the historical report.

The report evidence images are excluded from the packet. The reports contain the method-relevant
facts, and retaining the images would add story/UI disclosure without changing the method map.

### Prior research and adjudication

No prior research brief or report targets the playtest skill's method.

The repository contains product research briefs and a generic method-gap commissioning framework.
They are not predecessor lines because they do not identify this target or record a
playtest-method disposition.

Live GitHub searches for method gap, playtest methodology, the target path, and counterfactual
disclosure returned no target-method issue. Issues 97 and 98 are explicitly fenced product work:

- https://github.com/joeloverbeck/continuity-loom/issues/97
- https://github.com/joeloverbeck/continuity-loom/issues/98

## 5. Adopted-lineage fence

There is no adopted external research line for this target.

| Research line | Adopted capability | Current location | Version/date | Remaining limit |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| None — first external audit | Not applicable | Not applicable | 2026-07-18 | Do not mislabel organic skill iteration as prior external method research |

The following established baseline capabilities are not external-research adoptions, but the
researcher must not rediscover them under new names without credible evidence that they fail:

- pre-use intent and mental-model capture;
- contemporaneous observation and correction entries;
- visible-UI-only action and bounded recovery;
- blank-credential, provider-route, origin, and worktree custody guards;
- exact visible prompt extraction and fingerprinting;
- fresh cold-context response isolation;
- field-influence ledger, one retry, and one targeted counterfactual;
- human candidate editing and explicit acceptance;
- cumulative finding IDs and continuation handoff;
- privacy-safe evidence retention and a tested structural/privacy validator.

## 6. Settled-negative and run-scope fences

| Area | Permanent or this run | Reason | Reopen trigger or none |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Product defects and feature choices, including issues 97 and 98 | This run | They concern Continuity Loom behavior, not a missing instrument in the playtest method | A method claim depends on a product disposition that cannot be separated |
| Current dirty browser-driver and prompt-evaluation edits | This run | They are ordinary host/conformance improvements and are included as baseline, not gap proof | Evidence shows the resulting capability is still absent |
| Whether agent playtests predict real human author experience | Separate route | This is paired human transfer calibration, not method-gap research | Owner authorizes participant access and a paired human/agent study |
| Severity, usefulness, intervention, or confidence labels against known controls | Separate route | This is verdict calibration | A candidate recommendation survives this audit and the owner authorizes calibration fixtures |
| Mobile, alternate viewport, assistive-technology, radically different story task, or provider-result-card coverage | Separate route | These are out-of-distribution or specialist pressure tests | Owner declares one regime part of the operating envelope |
| No paid/OpenRouter calls during a playtest | Permanent policy for this target | Provider safety and cost boundary are explicit | Owner deliberately revises the target policy through normal skill change |
| Visible UI only; no API, DOM mutation, hidden state, database, or project-file advancement | Permanent policy for this target | It protects ecological validity and user-visible discoverability | None for this commission |
| Exactly one newly accepted segment per invocation | Permanent policy for this target | It is the declared local author-journey boundary | Owner changes the target's intended outcome |
| Full prompt, raw response, record payload, candidate prose, or accepted prose in durable reports | Permanent | Privacy and custody boundary | None |
| Rewriting, fixing, or adopting recommendations in the target | This run | Commissioning only maps and briefs research | A returned report is independently adjudicated through normal skill maintenance |

Do not silently reopen these areas. A researcher may identify an assumption or conflict at a
fence, but must route it rather than smuggling it into a recommendation.

## 7. Candidate gap areas

These are hypotheses. Challenge each one and check for equivalent existing machinery.

### 1. Staged blindness and instruction-contamination control

Local evidence: before first navigation, the operator has read a highly product-specific skill
that names the app's surfaces, expected coverage, assistance purposes, authority rules, and
completion route. The current pre-use mental-model entry records expectations but does not remove
that knowledge.

Hypothesis: the method needs a staged-blindness, role-separation, or contamination-accounting
instrument to warrant claims about onboarding, terminology, and unaided discoverability. Without
one, the instructed operator may systematically under-detect first-use failures.

Qualification:

- Intended outcome at stake: blind-first observation.
- Present coverage: mental-model capture and first-screen inspection, but no knowledge-exposure
  control.
- Material consequence: false confidence in discoverability or onboarding.
- External function to test: HCI first-use protocols, cognitive walkthrough role separation,
  experimental blinding, and observer-expectancy controls.
- Plausible fit: a short pre-instruction phase or independent cold first-view witness could fit the
  current browser and privacy envelope.
- Cheapest first witness to improve or reject: compare the current operator's first-view record
  with one genuinely cold, premise-only first-view observation on the same unopened app state.

### 2. Independent claim-evidence and rival-explanation review

Local evidence: one agent performs the journey, interprets it, assigns likely layer and severity,
selects evidence, and writes the report. The validator checks shape and selected privacy/custody
rules but not whether a screenshot, reproduction, and wording actually warrant the claim. The
first PRD-prep artifact narrowed one report inference about prompt highlighting after source/test
review, showing that an observation can remain useful while its explanation needs refinement.

Hypothesis: the method lacks a lightweight independent evidence-adjudication instrument for
high-consequence findings and strengths. That omission may allow observer error, implementation
inference, or confirmation bias to become durable backlog input.

Qualification:

- Intended outcome at stake: evidence-backed actionable findings.
- Present coverage: fact/interpretation separation, correction entries, confidence, diagnostics,
  and downstream prep.
- Material consequence: wrong product layer, exaggerated severity, or unnecessary work.
- External function to test: qualitative audit trails, negative-case analysis, triangulation,
  HCI observer reliability, and assurance claim-evidence review.
- Plausible fit: review only high-severity or decision-driving claims, with no raw story material.
- Cheapest first witness to improve or reject: give a cold reviewer three privacy-safe findings,
  their reproduction, and cited artifacts; require supported, contradicted, or insufficient plus
  one rival explanation before publication.

### 3. Stochastic prompt-evaluation design and uncertainty

Local evidence: a prompt normally receives one cold response, with one retry only after a
meaningfully poor result and one possible counterfactual. Both empirical runs observed a
structurally valid all-empty reconciliation response followed by many useful deltas from the same
prompt bytes. The skill correctly says one response is suggestive rather than proof, but report
verdicts and confidence remain single-evaluator judgments.

Hypothesis: the method needs a bounded repeated-measures, sequential-stopping, comparator, or
uncertainty instrument to distinguish prompt-contract weakness, stochastic model execution,
source insufficiency, and stable usefulness without turning every run into a benchmark suite.

Qualification:

- Intended outcome at stake: prompt usefulness and causal-layer attribution.
- Present coverage: cold isolation, unchanged-prompt retry, one-variable counterfactual, structured
  verdict, and explicit limitations.
- Material consequence: a one-off model miss may become product work, or a recurrent reliability
  hazard may be dismissed as noise.
- External function to test: evaluation science, experimental design, language-model evaluation,
  reliability analysis, metamorphic testing, and sequential sampling.
- Plausible fit: cold subagents are already available and paid provider calls remain forbidden.
- Cheapest first witness to improve or reject: on one naturally triggered high-impact prompt,
  predeclare a minimal sequential rule and compare its conclusion and cost with the current
  one-or-two-attempt rule. Actual verdict calibration remains a separately authorized route.

### 4. Natural-task portfolio coverage and saturation

Local evidence: each invocation covers one user-supplied story, one local segment, one browser,
and one 1440x900 viewport. Continuation deliberately follows only the supplied report, and each
report acknowledges its single-story limits. There is no target-level representation of which
in-envelope authoring situations, record mixes, assistance needs, or lifecycle transitions have
been naturally exercised.

Hypothesis: the skill needs a privacy-safe task-coverage and saturation instrument across natural
runs. Without one, repeated work may cluster around one story shape while untouched in-envelope
failure classes remain invisible.

Qualification:

- Intended outcome at stake: credible naturalistic coverage of the declared desktop author loop.
- Present coverage: within-run surface accounting and a story-local cumulative ledger.
- Material consequence: blind spots may persist even as report count grows.
- External function to test: usability task sampling, qualitative saturation, combinatorial test
  design, and coverage modeling.
- Plausible fit: store only coarse, non-story dimensions and never force irrelevant feature use.
- Cheapest first witness to improve or reject: define a small anonymized coverage card, apply it
  retrospectively to the two runs and prospectively to the next natural run, then test whether it
  reveals a decision-relevant blind spot.

### 5. Longitudinal method-feedback and revision triggers

Local evidence: the cumulative ledger is intentionally confined to one story chain. The first
method defect reached a target change through downstream PRD prep and skill-audit, but the package
does not declare when a report observation is methodology evidence, when repeated evidence
triggers skill review, or how a landing closes the loop without searching unrelated story reports.

Hypothesis: the method needs a lightweight, privacy-safe feedback instrument that records
method-level signals, revision triggers, witnesses, and adopted landings without turning story
reports into a global prompt corpus.

Qualification:

- Intended outcome at stake: learning from execution evidence and preventing repeated method
  failure.
- Present coverage: story-local cumulative ledger, Git history, downstream prep, and ordinary
  skill-audit.
- Material consequence: method weaknesses can recur, be mistaken for product defects, or be fixed
  without recoverable rationale.
- External function to test: implementation science, reliability learning systems, quality
  improvement, and lightweight decision records.
- Plausible fit: one small provenance marker or target-local method ledger, with no story prose or
  prompt content.
- Cheapest first witness to improve or reject: reconstruct the two runs, the counterfactual
  disclosure repair, and the two current reference edits into one minimal method-change card; test
  whether it changes the next audit or revision decision.

Also perform an open-ended sweep for consequential gap classes outside this list but inside the
declared scope. In particular, test whether the method's constructs of sincere, naturalistic,
useful, confidence, and intervention burden have missing instruments rather than merely imperfect
wording. Route any actual human-transfer or verdict-calibration exercise to its fenced workflow.

## 8. External knowledge bases

Investigate fields selected for functional relevance:

- Human-computer interaction and usability evaluation — staged first-use observation, task
  realism, cognitive walkthroughs, observer effects, evidence capture, and lightweight
  triangulation.
- Qualitative research methods — reflexivity, negative cases, audit trails, inter-observer review,
  saturation, and privacy-safe synthesis.
- Evaluation science and experimental design — comparators, replication, sequential stopping,
  confounding, uncertainty, and evidence-strength claims.
- Measurement and psychometrics — construct validity and operationalization of qualitative
  scales, while respecting the separate calibration fence.
- Software verification and testing — oracle problems, flaky/stochastic behavior, metamorphic
  relations, negative controls, and coverage models.
- Safety, assurance, reliability, and quality engineering — compact claim-evidence structures,
  escalation, containment, and learning from near misses.
- Implementation science and quality improvement — low-ceremony feedback loops, adoption
  witnesses, and revision triggers for a single-maintainer environment.

Add another field only when the open sweep justifies it. Borrow the smallest useful instrument
class; do not import a framework wholesale.

## 9. Research tasks

1. Reconstruct the skill's claim-instrument-evidence map from the exact packet.
2. Check whether each candidate is already covered under different terminology.
3. Identify consequential detection, validation, control, decision, or transfer failures.
4. Find relevant external instrument classes, supporting evidence, and contrary evidence.
5. Adapt credible instruments to the operating envelope and all settled fences.
6. Specify the cheapest first witness and required artifacts for each.
7. Give every recommendation an adopt now, defer with trigger, or reject disposition.
8. Perform the open-ended sweep.
9. Prioritize surviving recommendations as a coherent roadmap.
10. Explicitly separate method-gap findings from ordinary skill defects, product work, verdict
    calibration, paired human transfer calibration, policy choices, and out-of-distribution tests.

## 10. Recommendation contract

Every recommendation must state:

- **Gap:** the missing capability, independent of the solution.
- **Consequence:** what the skill cannot reliably know, distinguish, prevent, or transfer.
- **Current coverage:** why existing machinery does not already close it.
- **Prior art:** the external instrument or failure model with claim-level citations.
- **Adapted instrument:** the smallest coherent form that fits the operating envelope.
- **Assumptions and limits:** when it should work and what it still cannot establish.
- **Cost order:** one-time and recurring maintainer time, compute/tooling, participants, and
  dependencies; use orders of magnitude rather than false precision.
- **Cheapest first witness:** one trial-sized exercise, required artifacts, and observations for
  and against adoption.
- **Risks and dependencies:** ceremony, gaming, false assurance, duplication, maintenance, and new
  failure modes.
- **Disposition:** adopt now, defer with a concrete trigger, or reject, with reasons.
- **Landing target:** the proposed owning location if later adopted; this is not an edit.

Adopt now requires materiality, feasibility, and a proportionate witness. Defer requires a named
trigger. Reject covers out-of-scope, already-covered, unsupported, disproportionate, or
incompatible proposals. Interesting, future work, and consider are not dispositions.

## 11. Citation rules

Provide full bibliographic citations tied to supported claims. Prefer primary research, official
standards, and authoritative method sources. Mark an unverified source or claim
relayed—unverified. Distinguish sourced fact, expert consensus, analogy, and inference. Report
material contrary evidence and domain limits. Do not infer that an institutional practice is
useful here merely because it is common elsewhere.

The owner will independently verify every decision-critical citation before adoption.

## 12. Deliverable

Return one consolidated report containing:

1. scope, operating envelope, and current method map;
2. per-area findings with evidence and dispositions together;
3. the open-ended sweep;
4. already-covered and rejected candidates;
5. conflicts and dependencies between instruments;
6. a prioritized roadmap ordered by value, feasibility, dependency, and witness cost;
7. a citation-verification register, including every relayed—unverified claim; and
8. an appendix of candidate witnesses and expected artifacts.

Also include a route ledger that sends every non-method concern to ordinary skill iteration,
defect correction, verdict calibration, human transfer calibration, policy decision, or
out-of-distribution pressure testing as appropriate.

Do not return an unranked best-practices catalogue.

## 13. Authority and consumption boundary

Do not edit playtest, mark a recommendation adopted, decide product behavior, or settle policy for
the owner. Do not call OpenRouter, mutate the GitHub tracker, or inspect private /tmp story data.

After the report returns, the owner will:

1. independently verify every decision-critical citation;
2. adjudicate every recommendation and route every non-method concern;
3. run cheap witnesses where needed;
4. use skill-audit or the repository's normal skill-change process for any accepted revision;
5. rerun target-local tests and an appropriate playtest witness;
6. record adopted capabilities, rejected findings, deferred triggers, and the landing identity in
   the next lineage fence.

Research recommendations remain advice until that sequence is complete.

## 14. Completion condition

The audit is complete when every scoped area is reported, every recommendation satisfies the
contract, the open-ended sweep and negative findings are present, and the consolidated report is
delivered—or when a scoped area explicitly records that it yielded no defensible method gap.

The audit must not claim that bounded research proves the target has no method gaps.

### Exact executor handoff

Give the executor this instruction together with this entire brief:

~~~~text
Use reports/playtest-method-gap-research-brief-2026-07-18.md as the sole commission. Fetch every
base-ref source from the public Continuity Loom repository at commit
a3d6b4371f6f583f1964f414a4b34f76a12c22d1, decode and hash-check Appendices B and C as the two
current target overrides, then conduct the authorized external method research. Return one
consolidated Markdown report satisfying Sections 7 through 14. Do not edit the repository, invoke
OpenRouter, adopt recommendations, or treat product issues as method findings. Mark inaccessible or
unverified claims relayed—unverified.
~~~~

## Commission validation

- [x] Target skill, authority posture, version, content identity, and worktree state are clear.
- [x] Every named internal source is accessible through a public immutable ref or an inlined
  current supplement.
- [x] Every ref-backed path was verified at the named ref.
- [x] Outcomes, claims, instruments, evidence, thresholds, authority, failure, and feedback routes
  are mapped far enough to compare capabilities.
- [x] The operating envelope binds every recommendation.
- [x] Prior research lineage and in-flight work were censused.
- [x] Rejected, deferred, and separately routed areas have reopening rules.
- [x] Candidate areas arise from local evidence and remain hypotheses.
- [x] The open-ended sweep is mandatory.
- [x] External fields are selected by function.
- [x] Every recommendation owes prior art, adaptation, costs, a cheapest first witness, risks, a
  disposition, and a landing target.
- [x] Claim-level citation verification and relayed—unverified handling are explicit.
- [x] Negative findings, conflicts, dependencies, and a prioritized roadmap are required.
- [x] The executor has no authority to edit, adopt, or settle policy.
- [x] The post-report consumption route is explicit.
- [x] The brief makes no completeness claim.

## Appendix A — Target inventory and custody

Every target-local file was read completely. SHA-256 values identify the current bytes.

| Path | Disposition and role | Current SHA-256 / identity |
| --- | --- | --- |
| .claude/skills/playtest/SKILL.md | read — primary outcomes, non-negotiables, process, completion | e37314da2f286c884e1888d9e984bddc623d5a375f3537301feee7b5102ac4ac |
| references/run-setup.md | read — branch choice, custody, pre-use intent, one-segment boundary | e58dad08a132364377b6b4aa0c8fdf4f4883d5cfbb299d445229371c303bd917 |

| references/author-journey.md | read — new/continuation author loop, assistance, acceptance, light accessibility pass | ccc910c7a54abfb0237c8c3583febc633e3d76f1fdf0238deac90ac23638ae09 |
| references/prompt-evaluation.md | read — cold isolation, verdicts, field influence, retry, counterfactual; current dirty bytes in Appendix C | ce4478df805d1f4d6d3ba4bbc89ec2ca1509cc009a355df714ce15d7cb6df9fa |

| scripts/browser-session.mjs | read — fresh browser, provider/origin guards, diagnostics | bb119b7804ae089ef6decdbd7bfafda2c8d663a62da1b524e5da1b68ad817142 |

| scripts/browser-act.test.mjs | read — prompt-output and visible-clear controls | 5e4b742f7e3cab6ab461696f1dc6ba7d6961ee09ba7078a7b9747af67ce04be6 |

| scripts/validate-report.test.mjs | read — validator positive and negative controls | d53eded8bcd437871aa2aace28039acc07dc15605be1d27ed36c4903a53fe2c0 |

| Target-local templates, schemas, assets, agents metadata | excluded — none exist in the target package | not applicable |
| reports/assets/playtest-* | excluded — report summaries are sufficient; images add privacy exposure without changing the method map | not included |
| reports/playtest-the-unbidden-oath-2026-07-18T145754Z-prd-prep.md | excluded — untracked downstream product synthesis; not needed for target reconstruction or lineage | SHA-256 observed but deliberately not packet evidence |
| reports/method-gap-research-commissioning-framework.md | excluded — generic, untracked, and not target lineage | not included |
| Other skills and unrelated dirty worktree paths | excluded — outside the resolved target and method map | untouched |
